title: trevesia insidiator
intro: Trevesia insidiator, also known as the stinging caterpillar tree, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Southeast Asia. This plant is known for its toxic sap, which can cause skin irritation and allergic reactions. The sap of Trevesia insidiator has been found to have medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects, which can be used to treat various health conditions such as wounds and skin infections. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is prepared by making a paste from its leaves and applying it topically to treat skin conditions, and it can also be consumed as a tea to promote digestive health and reduce fever.
